close_reason: Explicit enum arms are clearer than wildcard for a 3-variant enum
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:193-213 has:
  - SigningAlgorithm::Ed25519 => return Err(...) // hand-crafted message
  - _ => return Err(...) // 'algorithm {:?} is not supported by AWS KMS'

The two arms differ only in error message verbosity. The wildcard catches any future SigningAlgorithm variant added to the non_exhaustive enum (Ed448, P-384 etc).

Readability concern: the future maintainer reading this will wonder why Ed25519 is special-cased when both arms produce the same SecretError::Backend variant. It is special-cased only for a more-specific error message.

Two equally good fixes:
  (a) Drop the Ed25519 arm and let the wildcard cover it; the {:?} message ('Ed25519 is not supported by AWS KMS') is just as informative.
  (b) Keep both arms but add a comment 'Ed25519 explicit so the error suggests valid alternatives; wildcard handles future variants without recompile-time review' — making the intent legible.

Mark opinion if you prefer the current shape.
title: Algorithm match has explicit Ed25519 arm and a wildcard fall-through with the same payload
